key_insight: Vitriflow turns melt-quench amorphous model generation into an explicit, material-specific decision workflow that calibrates simulation settings and screens artefacts to produce reproducible structure ensembles.
engineering_relevance: This gives computational materials engineers a practical way to choose melt-quench parameters and validation criteria based on the target material and structural question, rather than relying on fixed defaults.
method: Checks numerical stability before generating structures
Calibrates protocol choices using descriptors and user-defined artefact screening
Assesses statistical convergence in a material-specific descriptor space
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstrations on a-SiO2, a-Si3N4, and a-Sm2O3, including separation of defective silica, DFT-refinement response for nitride, and removal of recrystallised oxide structures.
watch_item: The workflow depends on descriptor quality and screening choices, so results may be sensitive to how the material-specific criteria are defined and tuned.
paper_title: Vitriflow: calibrated amorphous structure ensembles from melt-quench simulation
